title: MoneyPrinterTurbo Codebase Tour
description: A guided walkthrough of the AI-powered short video generation platform — covering architecture, services, API layer, web UI, and configuration

steps.title: Project Overview
steps.description: MoneyPrinterTurbo automatically generates short videos from a topic or keyword — orchestrating LLMs for scriptwriting, stock footage for visuals, text-to-speech for voice-over, and video processing for final assembly. The highlighted areas show the two entry points and three core directories.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the application directory to see where all backend logic lives

steps.title: Application Entry Points
steps.description: The highlighted modules bootstrap the backend server — wiring up CORS, static file serving, and the API router. A separate Streamlit app serves as the alternative entry point for the web interface.

interactiveActions.description: Click the ASGI module to see how the FastAPI app is configured

steps.title: Services — The Core Engine
steps.description:
The heart of the application. Each service handles one pipeline stage:

• LLM integration — 13+ providers for script and search-term generation
• Voice synthesis — text-to-speech via multiple engines
• Subtitle generation, material sourcing, and video composition
• Task orchestration — coordinates the full pipeline end to end
• State persistence — tracks progress in memory or Redis

interactiveActions.description: Click the task orchestrator to see how the full video generation pipeline is coordinated

steps.title: Controllers — API Endpoints
steps.description: The REST API layer. The highlighted endpoint group handles video generation, task queries, material uploads, and script generation. The task management layer provides in-memory and Redis-backed queues with concurrency control.

interactiveActions.description: Click the video endpoint module to see the main API routes

steps.title: Models — Data Schemas & Constants
steps.description:
Data structures used throughout the application:

• Schemas — Pydantic models for video parameters, aspect ratios, transition modes, and request/response contracts
• Constants — task states, supported file types, and punctuation rules
• Exceptions — custom error classes for HTTP responses and missing files

interactiveActions.description: Click the schema module to see the Pydantic models that define the API contract

steps.title: Web UI — Streamlit Interface
steps.description: A Streamlit-based web application offering a user-friendly alternative to the REST API. The highlighted entry point includes form inputs, real-time progress tracking, video preview, and an editable config panel. The internationalization layer supports 7 languages.

interactiveActions.description: Click the web UI entry point to explore the Streamlit interface

steps.title: Configuration & Resources
steps.description: The highlighted configuration template covers LLM keys, video source APIs, subtitle settings, Redis, and proxy options. Static resources include fonts for subtitles and copyright-free background music. Deployment files for Docker and dependencies are also shown.

interactiveActions.description: Click the configuration template to see all available options

steps.title: The Complete Picture
steps.description:
That's the full architecture — entry points, API controllers, pipeline services, data models, task management, web UI, and configuration.

The generation pipeline flows: Topic → Script → Search Terms → Stock Footage → Voice-over → Subtitles → Final Video.

interactiveActions.description: Explore the codebase freely — click on any building to view its source
